name: Phishing
description: Adversaries send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems

name: Spearphishing Attachment
description: Adversaries send spearphishing emails with a malicious attachment

name: Spearphishing Link
description: Adversaries send spearphishing emails with a malicious link

name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
description: Adversaries exploit a weakness in an internet-facing application

name: External Remote Services
description: Adversaries leverage external-facing remote services to access a network

name: Supply Chain Compromise
description: Adversaries manipulate products or delivery mechanisms prior to receipt by the end consumer

name: Valid Accounts
description: Adversaries obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts

name: Cloud Accounts
description: Adversaries obtain and abuse credentials of cloud accounts

name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
description: Adversaries abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands

name: PowerShell
description: Adversaries abuse PowerShell for execution and scripting

name: Exploitation for Client Execution
description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities in client applications

name: Scheduled Task/Job
description: Adversaries abuse task scheduling to establish persistence

name: Create Account
description: Adversaries create accounts to maintain access to victim systems

name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
description: Adversaries configure system settings to automatically execute a program during boot or logon

name: Abuse Elevation Control Mechanism
description: Adversaries circumvent mechanisms designed to control elevated privileges

name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities to elevate privileges

name: Access Token Manipulation
description: Adversaries modify access tokens to operate under a different security context

name: Impair Defenses
description: Adversaries maliciously modify components of a victim environment to hinder defenses

name: Indicator Removal
description: Adversaries delete or modify artifacts generated on a host system

name: Obfuscated Files or Information
description: Adversaries attempt to make payloads difficult to analyze by encoding or encrypting them

name: Brute Force
description: Adversaries use brute force techniques to attempt access when passwords are unknown

name: OS Credential Dumping
description: Adversaries attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login material

name: Steal Application Access Token
description: Adversaries steal application access tokens as a means of acquiring credentials

name: Unsecured Credentials
description: Adversaries search for common password storage locations to obtain user credentials

name: Account Discovery
description: Adversaries attempt to get a listing of valid accounts on a system or domain

name: Network Service Discovery
description: Adversaries attempt to get a listing of services running on remote hosts

name: Process Discovery
description: Adversaries attempt to get information about running processes

name: Remote Services
description: Adversaries use valid accounts to log into remote services for lateral movement

name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
description: Adversaries use alternate authentication material such as hashes or tickets

name: Archive Collected Data
description: Adversaries compress and encrypt data prior to exfiltration

name: Data from Local System
description: Adversaries search local system sources for data of interest prior to exfiltration

name: Data from Network Shared Drive
description: Adversaries search network shares for files of interest

name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
description: Adversaries steal data by exfiltrating it over an existing command and control channel

name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
description: Adversaries use legitimate external web services to exfiltrate data

name: Data Encrypted for Impact
description: Adversaries encrypt data on target systems to interrupt availability

name: Service Stop
description: Adversaries stop or disable services on a system to render those services unavailable

name: Network Denial of Service
description: Adversaries perform network denial of service to degrade or block availability

name: Application Layer Protocol
description: Adversaries communicate using OSI application layer protocols to avoid detection

name: Ingress Tool Transfer
description: Adversaries transfer tools or files from an external system into a compromised environment

name: Remote Access Software
description: Adversaries use legitimate remote access tools to establish an interactive C2 channel
